body:
dprint is a pluggable and configurable code formatting platform that unifies all your formatters.

This substantial release adds npm specifier plugins, brings dprint to many more CPU architectures, adds richer LSP support, and gives you finer-grained configuration with per-file overrides and config inheritance.

It includes two behaviour changes worth reading before you upgrade.

## Highlights

- 📦 **npm specifier plugins** — reference plugins with `npm:@scope/name@version`
- 🏗️ **Runs on many more architectures** — Windows on ARM, Android (Termux), ppc64le, and LoongArch
- 💡 **LSP completions & hover** for dprint configuration files
- 🔀 **`dprint fmt --dirty`** — format only the files with uncommitted git changes
- 🎛️ **Per-file plugin config overrides** — apply different plugin options to specific files
- 🧬 **`"inherit": true`** for nested configuration files
- ⚠️ **Plugin associations are now additive** (behaviour change)
- ⚠️ **Globs are now case-sensitive** (behaviour change)

We also have a new website: https://dprint.dev

## npm specifier plugins

You can now reference plugins via an `npm:` specifier in your config:

```jsonc
{
  "plugins": [
    "npm:@dprint/typescript@0.95.15",
    "npm:@dprint/json"
  ]
}
```

- Pinned form (`npm:@scope/name@version`) downloads and locks an exact version.
- Omitting the version (`npm:@scope/name`) resolves the plugin from `node_modules`, walking up from the config file so npm and your lockfile stay the source of truth.
- `dprint add npm:@scope/name` resolves the latest version and writes the pinned form, unless the package is in a nearby `package.json` under `devDependencies`, in which case the unversioned form is written, in which case node resolution will occur.

## More architectures supported

dprint now runs on a much wider range of CPU architectures, including:

- **Windows on ARM** (`aarch64-pc-windows-msvc`) - Now native—previously dprint was shipping the x64 binary.
- **Android / Termux**.
- **ppc64le**.
- **LoongArch** (now able to run plugins).

This is made possible by migrating the Wasm plugin runtime from Wasmer to [Wasmtime](https://wasmtime.dev/), with equivalent performance and a smaller dependency tree:

- Native Cranelift codegen on x86_64, aarch64, riscv64, and s390x.
- ppc64le, LoongArch, and Android compile to Wasmtime's portable Pulley bytecode (pure Rust, no native backend, no signal-based traps — which the Android sandbox doesn't allow), replacing the previous wasmi interpreter path.

## LSP completions and hover for config files

`dprint lsp` now provides autocompletion and hover documentation when editing `dprint.json` / `dprint.jsonc`, making it easier to discover plugins and configuration options without leaving your editor.

## `dprint fmt --dirty`

Format only the files with uncommitted changes in your git working directory — staged, unstaged, and untracked (but not gitignored):

```sh
dprint fmt --dirty
```

This complements the existing `--staged` flag.

## `"inherit": true` for nested config files

A nested configuration file can now opt in to inheriting its ancestor's plugins and configuration. Given a config at the repo root:

```jsonc
// ./dprint.json
{
  "typescript": {
    "indentWidth": 4
  },
  "plugins": [
    "https://plugins.dprint.dev/typescript-0.96.1.wasm"
  ]
}
```

A config in a subdirectory can inherit it with `"inherit": true`:

```jsonc
// ./sub-project/dprint.json
{
  "inherit": true,
  "typescript": {
    // inherits the ancestor's TypeScript config, but overrides the indent width
    "indentWidth": 2
  }
}
```

- Plugins in the nested config take precedence; any not specified are inherited.
- The ancestor's `includes` are **not** inherited.
- Inheriting is opt-in so that adding a config higher in the tree doesn't unexpectedly start affecting nested configs.

## Per-file plugin config overrides

Plugins now support `"overrides"` blocks to apply different configuration to specific files:

```jsonc
{
  "json": {
    "overrides": {
      "files": ["**/package.json", "**/composer.json"],
      "indentWidth": 4
    }
  }
}
```

Use an array for multiple overrides:

```jsonc
{
  "json": {
    "overrides": [
      {
        "files": ["**/package.json", "**/composer.json"],
        "indentWidth": 4
      },
      {
        "files": "**/special-package.json",
        "lineWidth": 80
      }
    ]
  }
}
```

Overrides are honoured consistently across CLI formatting, stdin, the editor service, the LSP, and host formatting. Note that overrides only change configuration — they don't include or exclude files.

## ⚠️ Plugin associations are now additive (behaviour change)

Previously, adding an `"associations"` glob to a plugin silently **replaced** the file extensions and file names it matched by default. Now associations are **additive**:

- A positive glob (e.g. `**/*.foo`) routes extra files to the plugin while its defaults keep matching.
- A negated glob (e.g. `!**/*.js`) cancels a default extension, file name, or path.

If you previously relied on a positive association to replace the defaults, add a negated glob to opt back out. Fixes the surprising behaviour in [#841](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/issues/841) and [#794](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/issues/794).

## ⚠️ Globs are now case-sensitive (behaviour change)

File pattern matching for `includes`/`excludes` globs is now case-sensitive ([#1082](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/issues/1082)). If your patterns relied on case-insensitive matching, update them to match the actual file casing.

## Read a list of files from stdin

The new `--stdin-files` flag reads a newline-separated list of file paths from stdin instead of from the command line arguments. It works with the `fmt`, `check`, `file-paths`, and `format-times` subcommands, which is handy when piping the output of another tool into dprint:

```sh
generate_files | dprint fmt --stdin-files
```

## Smarter `dprint init`

`dprint init` now scans the current directory and pre-selects the plugins whose files it finds, so the defaults match your project out of the box. The plugin picker scrolls to fit your terminal and supports type-to-filter, keeping it usable even when many plugins are available.

Pass `--yes` (`-y`) to skip the prompt entirely and accept those defaults — handy for scripts and CI:

```sh
dprint init --yes
```

The prompt is also skipped automatically when there's no interactive terminal.

## Other notable additions

- **`DPRINT_GLOBAL_GITIGNORE=1`** — opt in to respecting git's global excludes file (`core.excludesFile`). Opt-in because it's machine-specific and won't exist on CI.
- **`NO_COLOR` / `FORCE_COLOR`** support for controlling colored output.
- **`dprint config update --dry-run`** — preview config updates without writing.
- **`dprint resolved-config --file <path>`** — show only the plugins that would format a given file.
- **`dprint incremental-state`** — print the exact signal dprint uses to decide cache reuse, so you can diff it between revisions.
- **Shorter subcommand names** — `output-resolved-config`, `output-file-paths`, and `output-format-times` are now `resolved-config`, `file-paths`, and `format-times`.

---

## Changelog

### Features

- feat(BREAKING): make plugin associations additive to default file matching ([#1172](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1172))
- feat(BREAKING): make globs case sensitive ([#1089](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1089))
- feat: migrate wasm plugin runtime from wasmer to wasmtime ([#1178](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1178))
- feat: add Windows on ARM (aarch64-pc-windows-msvc) builds ([#1179](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1179))
- feat: add android (Termux) builds using the wasmi interpreter backend ([#1175](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1175))
- feat: add ppc64le builds using the wasmi interpreter backend ([#1174](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1174))
- feat: use LLVM backend for LoongArch to support plugins ([#1109](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1109))
- feat: LSP completions and hover for dprint configuration files ([#1177](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1177))
- feat: support resolving plugins via npm specifiers and node resolution ([#1134](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1134))
- feat: add `--dirty` flag to format git working directory changes ([#1171](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1171))
- feat: add per-file plugin config overrides ([#1136](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1136))
- feat: support `"inherit": true` in nested configuration files ([#1160](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1160))
- feat: opt-in support for git's global excludes file via `DPRINT_GLOBAL_GITIGNORE` ([#1163](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1163))
- feat: support reading a list of files to format from stdin ([#1157](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1157))
- feat: support `NO_COLOR` and `FORCE_COLOR` env vars ([#1155](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1155))
- feat: add `--dry-run` to `dprint config update` ([#1156](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1156))
- feat: add `--file` flag to `resolved-config` to filter plugins by file ([#1167](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1167))
- feat: add `dprint incremental-state` command ([#1149](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1149))
- feat: drop `output-` from some subcommands ([#1150](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1150))
- feat(npm): detect plugin kind on dprint add when no path is given ([#1183](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1183))
- feat(npm): add `--checksum` flag to dprint add ([#1184](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1184))
- feat(init): pre-select plugins from the current directory, scrollable/filterable picker, and `--yes` flag ([#1185](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1185))
- feat(init): scaffold plugin config from info.json (defaultConfig + configItems) ([#1186](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1186))
- feat(init): prioritize plugin pre-selection and surface it in the list
  ([#1187](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1187))

### Performance

- perf: reduce wasm plugin thread stack size to 4 MiB ([#1180](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1180))
- perf: read directories on multiple threads when globbing ([#1168](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1168))
- perf(windows): delay load DLLs not needed at startup ([#1164](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1164))
- perf: cache resolved global gitignore path to avoid repeated git subprocess ([#1165](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1165))
- perf: use `similar` for LSP diffing and drop `dissimilar` ([#1176](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1176))

### Bug Fixes

- fix: normalize LSP formatted line endings to match the editor document ([#1173](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1173))
- fix: avoid hang waiting for killed process plugins in `clear-cache` ([#1169](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1169))
- fix: kill process plugins when running `dprint clear-cache` ([#1154](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1154))
- fix(npm): download binary when optional dependency is omitted ([#1161](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1161))
- fix(npm): improve musl detection ([#1145](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1145))
- fix: recompile Wasm plugin from source when loading from cache fails ([#1158](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1158))
- fix: avoid regex size limit when given many literal file paths ([#1153](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1153))
- fix: read `.git/info/exclude` when respecting gitignore ([#1151](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1151))
- fix(plugins): surface plugin cache cleanup errors instead of swallowing them ([#1148](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1148))
- fix(core): prevent unbounded re-evaluation when conditions oscillate ([#1137](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1137))
- fix: include plugin's resolved config in incremental cache key ([#1138](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1138))
- fix(install.sh): correct riscv64 build name, add loongarch64 support, Windows `.exe` path, resolve relative `DPRINT_INSTALL` ([#1131](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1131))

### Internal

- refactor: redesign plugin cache as flat per-plugin sidecars ([#1181](https://github.com/dprint/dprint/pull/1181))

## Install

Run `dprint upgrade` or see https://dprint.dev/install/
